symptoms.symptom_name: Severe exertional crushing central chest tightness with partial relief and residual ache

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Exertional crushing chest pain that does not fully resolve is a hallmark of unstable angina/ACS.

symptoms.symptom_name: Radiation of chest pain to the left jaw

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Jaw radiation is a classic feature of cardiac ischaemic pain.

symptoms.symptom_name: Two-week crescendo pattern of new angina

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A crescendo pattern defines unstable angina, an acute coronary syndrome.

symptoms.symptom_name: Strong family history of premature MI/sudden cardiac death and high cholesterol

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Premature family history markedly raises pre-test probability of premature coronary disease.
Premature MI and high cholesterol clustering in the family supports an inherited lipid disorder.
Fatigue can accompany ischaemia but is non-specific; absence of syncope does not lower the ACS concern.

symptoms.symptom_name: Achilles tendon xanthomas, xanthelasma and corneal arcus at a young age

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Tendon xanthomas with corneal arcus in youth are characteristic stigmata of familial hypercholesterolaemia.

symptoms.symptom_name: Ischaemic ECG changes and/or elevated troponin

investigation_details.investigation_name: 12-lead ECG and serial troponin with continuous monitoring
investigation_details.expected_result: Ischaemic ECG changes and/or a rising troponin consistent with acute coronary syndrome
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: ACS cannot be diagnosed or risk-stratified and an evolving infarction would be missed.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: ECG changes and troponin elevation are the diagnostic markers of acute coronary syndrome.

symptoms.symptom_name: Severely elevated LDL cholesterol on fasting lipids

investigation_details.expected_result: Markedly elevated LDL cholesterol consistent with familial hypercholesterolaemia
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: The underlying familial hypercholesterolaemia goes unrecognised and untreated, perpetuating premature cardiovascular risk.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Severely elevated LDL supports the diagnosis of familial hypercholesterolaemia (confirmed with clinical criteria/genetics).

symptom_clusters.target_diagnosis: Acute coronary syndrome from premature coronary artery disease
symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Exertional crushing jaw-radiating chest tightness with a crescendo pattern and a strong premature-MI family history together indicate a high-probability acute coronary syndrome demanding an immediate ambulance and ECG/troponin, regardless of the patient's youth.

investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Immediate assessment for ischaemic changes/STEMI; must be done urgently in a monitored setting.
Detects myocardial injury and risk-stratifies acute coronary syndrome.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Fasting lipid profile (and later genetic testing)
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Confirms the underlying familial hypercholesterolaemia to guide lifelong lipid-lowering therapy - after the acute event.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Reassurance and next-day GP review without emergency assessment
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Deferring assessment of a possible evolving MI risks infarction, arrhythmia and sudden death.
